technical.title: Technical Analysis
technical.updatesEveryMinute: Technical indicators update every minute. These are mathematical tools, not crystal balls. They work best when multiple indicators agree. Always combine with your own research.
technical.overallBullish: {{count}} out of {{total}} indicators suggest the price will go UP. More tools are pointing to buying than selling — this is a positive sign, but nothing is guaranteed.
technical.overallBearish: {{count}} out of {{total}} indicators suggest the price will go DOWN. More tools are pointing to selling than buying — be cautious.
technical.overallNeutral: Indicators are split — {{bull}} say buy, {{bear}} say sell, {{neutral}} are undecided. The market is uncertain. Best to wait for a clearer signal.

trend.title: Trend Direction
trend.explain: Is the Gold price going up, down, or sideways? We check over different time windows. An ascending trend means higher highs — like climbing stairs. Descending means falling. Sideways means the price is stuck in a range.
trend.whatShort: What happened in the last ~1 day
trend.whatMedium: What happened in the last ~2 days
trend.whatLong: What happened in the last ~4 days
trend.ascendingDesc: {{what}}. Price is climbing — each high is higher than the last. Good for buyers.
trend.descendingDesc: {{what}}. Price is falling — each low is lower than the last. Sellers are in control.
trend.sidewaysDesc: {{what}}. Price is flat — no clear direction. The market is waiting for something to happen.

rsi.title: RSI (Relative Strength Index)
rsi.explain: Think of RSI like a speedometer for buyers vs sellers. It goes from 0 to 100. Above 70 = too many buyers (overbought, price likely to drop). Below 30 = too many sellers (oversold, price likely to bounce). Between 30-70 = normal.
rsi.standardDesc: The most commonly used RSI. This is what most traders look at.
rsi.fastDescOverbought: Looks at the last 7 hours only. Quick reactions — showing overbought, short-term drop likely.
rsi.fastDescOversold: Looks at the last 7 hours only. Quick reactions — showing oversold, short-term bounce likely.
rsi.fastDescNeutral: Looks at the last 7 hours only. Quick reactions — nothing extreme right now.
rsi.slowDescOverbought: Looks at the last 30 hours. Slower to react but more reliable — even the slow RSI says overbought, strong sell signal.
rsi.slowDescOversold: Looks at the last 30 hours. Slower to react but more reliable — even the slow RSI says oversold, strong buy signal.
rsi.slowDescNeutral: Looks at the last 30 hours. Slower to react but more reliable — no extreme reading.
rsi.explainHeavilyOverbought: At {{value}}, Gold is heavily overbought. Too many people bought recently — price often drops after this. Think of it like a rubber band stretched too far.
rsi.explainOverbought: At {{value}}, Gold is overbought. Buyers are running out of steam. A price pullback is likely soon.
rsi.explainMildlyBullish: At {{value}}, buyers have slight control. Momentum is mildly positive — the price is leaning upward but nothing extreme.
rsi.explainBalanced: At {{value}}, the market is balanced. Neither buyers nor sellers are in control — price could go either way.
rsi.explainMildlyBearish: At {{value}}, sellers have slight control. Momentum is mildly negative — the price is drifting down slowly.
rsi.explainOversold: At {{value}}, Gold is oversold. Too many people sold recently — this is often a good buying opportunity.
rsi.explainExtremelyOversold: At {{value}}, Gold is extremely oversold. Panic selling has pushed it very low — historically the price bounces back from here.

macd.title: MACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence)
macd.explain: MACD measures the momentum (speed) of price changes. It compares fast and slow moving averages. When the MACD line crosses above the signal line, it's a buy signal. When it crosses below, it's a sell signal. The histogram shows the difference — positive = bullish push, negative = bearish push.
macd.signalDesc: A smoothed version of MACD. When MACD crosses above this line = buy. Below = sell.
macd.lineDescPositive: Positive — the fast average is above the slow average, meaning recent price action is stronger than the longer trend. Bullish.
macd.lineDescNegative: Negative — the fast average is below the slow average, meaning recent price action is weaker than the longer trend. Bearish.
macd.histExplainStrongUp: Very strong upward push. Buyers are in full control and the price is rising with strong momentum.
macd.histExplainUp: Positive momentum — buyers are winning. The price trend is moving upward. The bigger this number, the stronger the push.
macd.histExplainDown: Negative momentum — sellers are winning. The price trend is moving downward. This suggests continued falling prices.
macd.histExplainStrongDown: Very strong downward push. Sellers are in full control and price is dropping fast.

movingAverages.title: Moving Averages
movingAverages.explain: A moving average smooths out the price over time. If today's price is ABOVE the average, it means Gold is doing better than its recent history (bullish). If BELOW, it's doing worse (bearish). Shorter averages react faster, longer ones show the big picture.
movingAverages.aboveBy: Price is ${{amount}} ABOVE this average — bullish.
movingAverages.belowBy: Price is ${{amount}} BELOW this average — bearish.
movingAverages.ema9Desc: Average price over last 9 hours. Very fast — shows what happened today.
movingAverages.ema21Desc: Average price over last 21 hours (~1 day). Good for short-term trends.
movingAverages.sma20Desc: Simple average of last 20 hours. A common benchmark traders watch closely.
movingAverages.ema50Desc: Average price over last 50 hours (~2 days). Medium-term trend direction.
movingAverages.sma111Desc: Average price over ~5 days. Used in the Pi Cycle indicator to spot major tops.
movingAverages.ema200Desc: Average over ~8 days. THE most important line in trading — price above = bull market, below = bear market.
movingAverages.sma200Desc: The "golden line". If Gold is above this, the long-term trend is healthy.
movingAverages.sma350Desc: Average over ~15 days. Part of the Pi Cycle Top indicator for spotting market tops.

bollinger.title: Bollinger Bands
bollinger.explain: Imagine two rubber bands around the price — one above, one below. The price usually stays between them. When it touches the top band, it's expensive (might drop). When it touches the bottom band, it's cheap (might rise). When the bands squeeze together, a big move is coming.
bollinger.position: Where is price in the bands?
bollinger.upperDesc: The upper limit of normal price range. If price touches this, it may be overextended.
bollinger.middleDesc: The 20-period average price. Think of this as the 'fair value' center.
bollinger.lowerDesc: The lower limit of normal price range. If price touches this, it may be a bargain.
bollinger.widthWide: Bands are WIDE — high volatility. Price is making big moves. Expect continued swings.
bollinger.widthNarrow: Bands are NARROW (squeeze) — low volatility. A big price explosion is likely coming soon, but direction is unknown.
bollinger.widthNormal: Bands are normal width. Moderate volatility — nothing extreme.
bollinger.posNearTop: Price is near the top of its normal range ({{pct}}%). Like a ball bouncing between walls — it tends to come back down.
bollinger.posNearBottom: Price is near the bottom of its normal range ({{pct}}%). Like a ball that bounced off the floor — it tends to go back up.
bollinger.posMiddle: Price is in the middle of its normal range ({{pct}}%). No extreme position — could go either way.
bollinger.bandsWide: Bands are wide = high volatility (big price swings expected).
bollinger.bandsNarrow: Bands are narrow = low volatility (a big move is building up, direction unknown).

volume.title: Volume Analysis
volume.explain: Volume = how much Gold is being traded. High volume means many people are buying/selling (strong conviction). Low volume means few people are trading (weak moves that can reverse easily). Volume confirms trends — a price move WITH high volume is trustworthy.
volume.vwapAbove: Price is ABOVE VWAP — means the average buyer today is in profit. Bullish. Traders see this as a sign of strength.
volume.vwapBelow: Price is BELOW VWAP — means the average buyer today is at a loss. Bearish. Traders see this as weakness.
volume.vwapDefault: The average price weighted by volume — shows where the "true" average trading price is.
volume.obvDesc: Running total of volume: adds volume on up days, subtracts on down days. If OBV is rising, smart money is accumulating (buying). If falling, they're distributing (selling).
volume.ratioVeryHigh: Trading volume is {{ratio}}x the average — VERY high activity. Whatever direction the price is moving, lots of people agree. This move has strong conviction.
volume.ratioHigh: Trading volume is {{ratio}}x the average — above normal. More people are trading than usual, which gives the current price move more credibility.
volume.ratioNormal: Trading volume is {{ratio}}x the average — normal. Nothing unusual happening in terms of trading activity.
volume.ratioLow: Trading volume is {{ratio}}x the average — below normal. Fewer people are trading. Price moves right now are less trustworthy.
volume.ratioVeryLow: Trading volume is very low at {{ratio}}x average. Almost nobody is trading. Any price move right now is unreliable and can easily reverse.

supportResistance.title: Support & Resistance
supportResistance.explain: Support = a price level where Gold stops falling (like a floor — buyers step in). Resistance = a price level where it stops rising (like a ceiling — sellers step in). These levels help predict where price will bounce or get rejected.
supportResistance.resistanceDesc: Gold may struggle to go above {{price}}. If it breaks through, that's very bullish — the next move up could be strong. Price is {{distanceText}}.
supportResistance.resistanceDescDefault: The estimated price ceiling based on recent highs and lows.
supportResistance.resistanceAbove: already above this level — bullish breakout
supportResistance.pivotDesc: The balance point calculated from yesterday's high, low, and close. Above pivot = bullish day, below = bearish day. Traders use this to judge the overall mood.
supportResistance.supportDesc: Gold should find buyers around {{price}}. If it breaks below, that's bearish — more selling could follow. Price is {{distanceText}}.
supportResistance.supportDescDefault: The estimated price floor based on recent highs and lows.
supportResistance.supportBelow: dangerously close to or below this support

trendStrength.title: Trend Strength
trendStrength.explain: Is the current trend strong or weak? ADX measures this. It doesn't tell you the direction (up/down), only HOW STRONG the move is. Below 20 = no real trend (choppy market). Above 25 = definite trend. Above 50 = very strong trend.
trendStrength.adxVeryStrong: ADX is {{value}} — very strong trend. Whatever direction Gold is going, it's moving there with conviction. Don't fight this trend.
trendStrength.adxClear: ADX is {{value}} — a clear trend exists. The price is consistently moving in one direction. This is a good time for trend-following strategies.
trendStrength.adxBorderline: ADX is {{value}} — borderline. A trend might be forming but it's not confirmed yet. Wait for it to go above 25 for more certainty.
trendStrength.adxNoTrend: ADX is {{value}} — no clear trend. The market is choppy and directionless. Price is bouncing randomly. Not a great time to trade trends.
trendStrength.atrDesc: Gold's average price swing is ${{value}} per hour. This tells you how much the price typically moves — useful for setting stop-losses. Bigger = more volatile.
trendStrength.volatilityHigh: HIGH volatility — price is swinging wildly. Bigger potential profits but also bigger risks. Be extra careful with positions.
trendStrength.volatilityModerate: MODERATE volatility — normal market conditions. Reasonable price swings.
trendStrength.volatilityLow: LOW volatility — very calm market. Small price movements. A breakout might be approaching.

btcDominance.title: Gold Dominance
btcDominance.explain: Gold Dominance shows what percentage of the TOTAL precious metals market belongs to Gold. When dominance rises, money flows FROM other metals TO Gold (people trust Gold more). When it falls, money flows TO other metals (people are taking more risk). High dominance = safer market.
btcDominance.ethDominanceDesc: Ethereum's share of the precious metals market. When ETH dominance rises, it often signals growing interest in ETFs and other metals.
btcDominance.totalMarketGrew: The entire precious metals market grew in the last 24 hours — money is coming into precious metals overall.
btcDominance.totalMarketShrank: The entire precious metals market shrank — money is leaving precious metals. Even strong coins can fall in this environment.
btcDominance.gaugeHigh: At {{value}}%, Gold dominates the precious metals market. Money is flowing into Gold — this usually means people are playing it safe. Bullish for Gold specifically.
btcDominance.gaugeNormal: At {{value}}%, Gold has a moderate market share. Neither extreme — normal conditions.
btcDominance.gaugeLow: At {{value}}%, Gold's share is low. Money is flowing into other metals — this is called "other metals rally". Can mean traders are more risk-hungry.

stochRsi.title: Stochastic RSI
stochRsi.explain: This is like RSI on steroids — it measures the RSI of the RSI, making it more sensitive. It swings between 0 and 100 very quickly. Below 20 = extremely oversold (buy opportunity). Above 80 = extremely overbought (sell signal). The %K and %D lines crossing is the actual trade signal.
stochRsi.kFastDesc: The fast-reacting line. When it enters extreme zones (above 80 or below 20), pay attention.
stochRsi.dSlowDescAbove: The fast line (%K) is ABOVE the slow line (%D) — this is a buy signal. Momentum is shifting upward.
stochRsi.dSlowDescBelow: The fast line (%K) is BELOW the slow line (%D) — this is a sell signal. Momentum is shifting downward.
stochRsi.gaugeOverbought: At {{value}}, Gold is extremely overbought in the short term. This indicator reacts fast — a quick pullback is very likely. Short-term traders would sell here.
stochRsi.gaugeOversold: At {{value}}, Gold is extremely oversold in the short term. This often marks a local bottom. Short-term traders would look to buy here.
stochRsi.gaugeNeutral: At {{value}}, no extreme reading. The short-term momentum is neutral — wait for it to reach the zones above 80 or below 20 for clearer signals.

williamsR.title: Williams %R
williamsR.explain: Williams %R tells you where the closing price is relative to the highest high over the last 14 periods. It ranges from -100 (lowest point) to 0 (highest point). Below -80 = oversold (price is near its recent low — might bounce). Above -20 = overbought (near its recent high — might drop).
williamsR.gaugeOversold: At {{value}}, the price is near its recent LOWEST point. Historically, this is where bounces happen. Think of it as a sale — Gold is trading at the bottom of its recent range.
williamsR.gaugeOverbought: At {{value}}, the price is near its recent HIGHEST point. It may struggle to go higher and could pull back. Think of it as Gold being "expensive" relative to recent prices.
williamsR.gaugeNeutral: At {{value}}, the price is in the middle of its recent range. Neither cheap nor expensive right now.

ichimoku.title: Ichimoku Cloud
ichimoku.explain: A Japanese trading system that shows support, resistance, momentum, and trend direction all at once. The 'cloud' is a shaded zone between two lines. Price ABOVE the cloud = bullish (strong uptrend). BELOW = bearish. INSIDE = no clear trend. The Tenkan/Kijun cross gives buy/sell signals.
ichimoku.aboveCloud: Price is ABOVE the cloud — Strong Bullish. The cloud below acts as a safety net (support). This is the best position to be in as a buyer.
ichimoku.belowCloud: Price is BELOW the cloud — Strong Bearish. The cloud above acts as a ceiling (resistance). Sellers are in control and it's hard for price to recover.
ichimoku.insideCloud: Price is INSIDE the cloud — Uncertain. The market is in transition between bullish and bearish. Wait for a clear breakout above or below the cloud.
ichimoku.tenkanAbove: Price is above the conversion line — short-term momentum is up. Like having the wind at your back.
ichimoku.tenkanBelow: Price is below the conversion line — short-term momentum is down. Buyers are struggling.
ichimoku.tenkanDefault: The short-term trend line (average of 9-period high and low).
ichimoku.kijunAbove: Price is above the base line — medium-term trend is bullish. This is the more important of the two lines.
ichimoku.kijunBelow: Price is below the base line — medium-term trend is bearish. This is a stronger bearish signal than being below Tenkan.
ichimoku.kijunDefault: The medium-term trend line (average of 26-period high and low).
ichimoku.cloudTopDesc: The top edge of the cloud. Acts as support when price is above the cloud, or resistance when below.
ichimoku.cloudBottomDesc: The bottom edge of the cloud. This is the stronger support/resistance level.

candlestick.title: Candlestick Patterns
candlestick.explain: Each 'candle' on a chart shows the opening price, closing price, highest price, and lowest price for a time period. Certain shapes have names and predict what comes next. Like reading tea leaves — but these patterns have been used by traders for centuries.
candlestick.noPatterns: No special patterns detected on the latest candle. The price action is unremarkable right now — no strong reversal signals.
candlestick.dojiDesc: The candle opened and closed at almost the same price. This means INDECISION — buyers and sellers are equally matched. Often appears right before a reversal.
candlestick.hammerDesc: Price dropped significantly but buyers pushed it back up. Shaped like a hammer. This is a BULLISH reversal signal — sellers tried to push price down but failed.
candlestick.invertedHammerDesc: Price rose significantly but sellers pushed it back down, then buyers recovered some. Can signal a BOTTOM is forming — next candle confirms.
candlestick.bullishEngulfingDesc: Today's green candle completely "swallowed" yesterday's red candle. This is a STRONG BUY signal — buyers have completely overwhelmed sellers.
candlestick.bearishEngulfingDesc: Today's red candle completely "swallowed" yesterday's green candle. This is a STRONG SELL signal — sellers have completely overwhelmed buyers.
candlestick.morningStarDesc: A 3-candle pattern: big red candle, tiny candle (indecision), then big green candle. Like dawn after a dark night — signals the downtrend is ENDING and price will rise.
candlestick.eveningStarDesc: A 3-candle pattern: big green candle, tiny candle, then big red candle. Like sunset — signals the uptrend is ENDING and price will fall.
candlestick.bodySizeLarge: Large body — strong conviction in the direction. Buyers or sellers clearly won this candle.
candlestick.bodySizeMedium: Medium body — moderate move. Neither side dominated strongly.
candlestick.bodySizeSmall: Tiny body — almost no difference between open and close. The market is undecided.
candlestick.upperShadowLong: Long upper shadow — sellers pushed the price down from the high. There's selling pressure above current price.
candlestick.upperShadowShort: Short upper shadow — not much selling pressure above. Buyers stayed in control.
candlestick.lowerShadowLong: Long lower shadow — buyers pushed the price up from the low. There are buyers waiting below, providing support.
candlestick.lowerShadowShort: Short lower shadow — price didn't dip much. No significant buying interest at lower prices.

advanced.title: Advanced Metrics
advanced.explain: These are specialized tools used by experienced traders to spot major market turning points. They look at longer-term patterns that can signal whether Gold is at a cycle top (time to sell) or cycle bottom (time to buy).
advanced.mayerExtreme: At {{value}}, Gold is MORE THAN 2.4x its 200-period average. Historically, this means it's dangerously overheated — major corrections have followed. Extreme caution.
advanced.mayerHigh: At {{value}}, Gold is well above its long-term average. It's doing great but getting expensive. Not the best time to buy new positions.
advanced.mayerAbove: At {{value}}, Gold is above its long-term average — healthy and positive. This is a normal bull market reading.
advanced.mayerBelow: At {{value}}, Gold is slightly below its long-term average. It's relatively cheap compared to history — could be a buying opportunity.
advanced.mayerDeep: At {{value}}, Gold is significantly below its long-term average. Historically, buying at this level has been very profitable long-term.
advanced.piCycleDanger: At {{value}}, this is approaching 1.0 — DANGER ZONE. The Pi Cycle indicator has historically predicted every major Gold top within 3 days when this hits 1.0.
advanced.piCycleWarming: At {{value}}, getting warmer but not critical yet. The Pi Cycle top signal triggers when this reaches 1.0. Still some room to grow.
advanced.piCycleSafe: At {{value}}, well below the danger zone. Gold still has significant room before hitting a Pi Cycle top signal. The cycle has more room to run.
advanced.goldenCross: The 50-period average is ABOVE the 200-period average ({{value}}). This is called a "Golden Cross" — one of the most famous bullish signals in all of trading. It means the short-term trend is outperforming the long-term trend. Historically, big rallies follow.
advanced.deathCross: The 50-period average is BELOW the 200-period average ({{value}}). This is called a "Death Cross" — one of the most famous bearish signals. The short-term trend is underperforming. Historically, this precedes further declines.
advanced.zScoreHigh: At {{value}} standard deviations above average — price has stretched TOO FAR above normal. Like a rubber band, it tends to snap back. Expect a pullback toward the average.
advanced.zScoreLow: At {{value}} standard deviations below average — price has dropped TOO FAR below normal. Like a rubber band, it tends to snap back UP. Good buying opportunity.
advanced.zScoreNormal: At {{value}} standard deviations from average — within normal range. No extreme stretching in either direction.

rateOfChange.title: Rate of Change
rateOfChange.explain: Simply: how much did the price change over different time periods? Positive = price went up. Negative = price went down. Bigger numbers = faster movement. This tells you the SPEED of the price change.
rateOfChange.upSignificant: Gold went UP {{pct}}% over {{period}}. That's a significant move — strong buying pressure.
rateOfChange.upModerate: Gold went UP {{pct}}% over {{period}}. A moderate gain.
rateOfChange.upSmall: Gold went UP {{pct}}% over {{period}}. A small gain.
rateOfChange.downSignificant: Gold went DOWN {{pct}}% over {{period}}. That's a significant drop — strong selling pressure.
rateOfChange.downModerate: Gold went DOWN {{pct}}% over {{period}}. A moderate decline.
rateOfChange.downSmall: Gold went DOWN {{pct}}% over {{period}}. A small decline.

indicatorHistory.title: Indicator History
indicatorHistory.explain: How key indicators have changed over time. RSI trending from 30 to 72 tells a story that a single snapshot at 72 misses.

technical.btcDominanceDescription: Gold Dominance shows what percentage of the TOTAL precious metals market belongs to Gold. When dominance rises, money flows FROM other metals TO Gold (people trust Gold more). When it falls, money flows TO other metals (people are taking more risk). High dominance = safer market.
technical.stochRsiDescription: This is like RSI on steroids — it measures the RSI of the RSI, making it more sensitive. It swings between 0 and 100 very quickly. Below 20 = extremely oversold (buy opportunity). Above 80 = extremely overbought (sell signal). The %K and %D lines crossing is the actual trade signal.
technical.williamsRDescription: Williams %R tells you where the closing price is relative to the highest high over the last 14 periods. It ranges from -100 (lowest point) to 0 (highest point). Below -80 = oversold (price is near its recent low — might bounce). Above -20 = overbought (near its recent high — might drop).
technical.ichimokuDescription: A Japanese trading system that shows support, resistance, momentum, and trend direction all at once. The 'cloud' is a shaded zone between two lines. Price ABOVE the cloud = bullish (strong uptrend). BELOW = bearish. INSIDE = no clear trend. The Tenkan/Kijun cross gives buy/sell signals.
technical.candlestickDescription: Each 'candle' on a chart shows the opening price, closing price, highest price, and lowest price for a time period. Certain shapes have names and predict what comes next. Like reading tea leaves — but these patterns have been used by traders for centuries.
technical.advancedDescription: These are specialized tools used by experienced traders to spot major market turning points. They look at longer-term patterns that can signal whether Gold is at a cycle top (time to sell) or cycle bottom (time to buy).
technical.rateOfChangeDescription: Simply: how much did the price change over different time periods? Positive = price went up. Negative = price went down. Bigger numbers = faster movement. This tells you the SPEED of the price change.

powerLaw.title: Power Law Model
powerLaw.explain: The Power Law model maps Gold's price trajectory based on adoption growth patterns. It provides a fair value corridor — prices below the band suggest undervaluation, above suggest overvaluation.
powerLaw.disclaimer: The Power Law model is a mathematical framework, not a price prediction. Past patterns may not continue.
powerLaw.description: The Gold Power Law model describes Gold's long-term price trajectory using a power law relationship: Price = 10^(-17.016 + 5.845 * log10(days))
powerLaw.howItWorks: How it works: Gold's price follows a straight line on a log-log chart, with R² > 0.95 over its entire history. The model provides a "fair value" corridor — price tends to oscillate between support (0.42x) and resistance (1.5x) of the fair value line.
powerLaw.limitations: Limitations: Past performance doesn't guarantee future results. The model assumes continued adoption growth and doesn't account for black swan events or regulatory changes.

elliott.title: Elliott Wave Analysis
elliott.explain: Elliott Wave Theory identifies repetitive wave patterns in market prices. Markets move in 5 waves in the direction of the trend (impulse), followed by 3 corrective waves.
elliott.disclaimer: Wave counts are subjective and may change as new price data arrives.
elliott.theoryDescription: Elliott Wave Theory identifies recurring fractal wave patterns in market prices. Markets move in 5-wave impulses (with the trend) and 3-wave corrections (against the trend).
elliott.threeRules: Three inviolable rules: (1) Wave 2 never retraces more than 100% of Wave 1. (2) Wave 3 is never the shortest impulse wave. (3) Wave 4 never enters Wave 1 price territory.
elliott.fibRatios: Fibonacci ratios guide wave projections: Wave 3 often extends to 1.618x of Wave 1, Wave 4 retraces to 0.382x of Wave 3, and Wave 5 often equals Wave 1 in length.
elliott.divergenceInfo: Divergences (price vs RSI/MACD) at swing points help confirm wave completions, especially at Wave 5 endings.
elliott.summaryImpulse: Gold is in Wave {{wave}} of a {{direction}} impulse pattern.
elliott.summaryCorrective: Gold is in Wave {{wave}} of a {{direction}} corrective pattern.
elliott.summaryUnclear: Elliott Wave pattern is unclear. More data needed for clear wave count.

liquidations.title: Liquidation Map
liquidations.explain: Liquidation data shows where leveraged positions will be force-closed. Clusters of liquidations act as price magnets — price often moves toward them to trigger cascading liquidations.
liquidations.disclaimer: Liquidation data is estimated and may not reflect exact exchange figures.
liquidations.balancedDesc: Market is balanced. No strong liquidation bias.
liquidations.longHeavyDesc: {{pct}}% longs — a dip could trigger cascading long liquidations.
liquidations.shortHeavyDesc: {{pct}}% shorts — a pump could trigger a short squeeze.
liquidations.extremeFundingDesc: Extreme positive funding = crash risk. Negative = squeeze potential.

howToUse.paragraph1: Liquidation clusters act as price magnets. Market makers and whales deliberately push price toward large clusters to trigger cascading liquidations, creating rapid moves.
howToUse.paragraph2: Red bars = long liquidations (below price). If price drops to these levels, leveraged longs get force-closed, accelerating the dump. Green bars = short liquidations (above price). A move up triggers short squeezes.
howToUse.paragraph3: Pro tip: When funding rate and L/S ratio diverge from top trader positions, it often signals an incoming liquidation event. Watch the Trading Insights section above.

liquidations.insightFundingHigh: High positive funding ({{rate}}%) — longs are paying premium. Price may correct down.
liquidations.insightFundingNegative: Negative funding ({{rate}}%) — shorts are paying. Potential squeeze upward.
liquidations.insightSmartMoney: Smart money divergence: Retail is {{retailBias}} ({{lsRatio}}) while top traders are {{smartBias}} ({{topRatio}}). Follow the smart money.
liquidations.insightLongCluster: Long liquidation cluster just {{pct}}% below price (${{volume}}). A dip could cascade.
liquidations.insightShortCluster: Short squeeze zone just {{pct}}% above price (${{volume}}). A pump could cascade.

events.title: Event Memory
events.subtitle: Historical pattern analysis
events.heatmapDesc: Average Gold price change after events by category and timeframe
events.explain: Historical event tracking shows how market events affected Gold price across different timeframes.
events.systemDescription: The Event Memory system tracks market events and measures their actual price impact across multiple timeframes. When similar events happen again, you'll know the historical pattern.
events.noEventsYet: No events tracked yet. Events will appear as they are detected.

history.title: Prediction History
history.calibrationDesc: Higher confidence should = higher accuracy. Dots sized by sample count.

whales.title: Whale Watcher
whales.noWhales: No whale transactions found for this filter

addressDetail.title: Address Detail
addressDetail.noTxs: No whale transactions found for this address
